rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM R. PARKER et al VS. ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANTS ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS LP, AND ASTRAZENECA, LP'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute. The motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is granted. Plaintiffs have a duty to proceed with reasonable diligence in the prosecution of their actions. (See CCP § 583.130.) It is within the court's discretion to dismiss an action where appropriate. (See CCP § 583.410.) Here, Plaintiffs delayed for over three years before re-filing their action in the suitable forum. Therefore dismissal is appropriate under the circumstances. (See CCP § 583.420(a)(2)(A).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

It is within the court's discretion to dismiss an action where appropriate. (See CCP § 583.410.) Here, Plaintiffs delayed for over three years before re-filing their action in the suitable forum. Therefore dismissal is appropriate under the circumstances. (See CCP § 583.420(a)(2)(A).) rulings.Case Title: DIDIER MARTINEZ RAMOS VS. MOHAMMED ARIF ARMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgement On Mohammed Arif Arman Dba Dailey Dollar
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT MOHAMMED ARMAN'S Motion To Vacate Judgement On Mohammed Arif Arman dba Dailey Dollar. The motion is denied. Defendant Arman fails to demonstrate that he actually served the motion. Moreover, as to the merits, defendant fails to demonstrate just cause for relief. There is no declaration from counsel indicating that he did not have knowledge of the trial date. Any error of counsel is imputed to defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: DIDIER MARTINEZ RAMOS VS. MOHAMMED ARIF ARMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgement On Hanif Amerzada Dba Nilare Dollar Only
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT HANIF AMERZADA'S Motion To Vacate Judgement On Hanif Amerzada dba Nilare Dollar Only. The motion is granted. First, the motion is timely under CCP 473.5. Second, defendant Amerzada's declaration demonstrates his lack of actual notice in time to defend the action. Third, defendant provides a proposed answer. Fourth, there is a policy to hear cases on the merits. Defendant's proof of service indicates timely service of the motion and plaintiff has addressed the merits of the motion. "It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. This rule applies even when no notice was given at all." Carlton v. Quint, (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALLIANCE CREDIT SERVICES INC. VS. MARTIN ENG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Deem Admitted And To Compel Discovery Responses And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday June 24, 2015, Line 7 - PLAINTIFF ALLIANCE CREDIT SERVICES INC.'s Motion For Order To Deem Admitted And To Compel Discovery Responses And Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Margaret Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off calendar. Insufficient notice pursuant to CCP sec. 1005(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to margaret.niver@pillsburylaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BOOKER PAGE et al VS. MARK DAWSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF. The motion for summary judgment is denied because defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. The existence of an employment relationship is a factual issue. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of an employment relationship between Mr. Dawson and moving defendants and the factors highlighted in S.G. Borello & Sons, Inc. v. State Dept. of Indus Relations, (1989) 48 Cal.3d. 341, 350. Uber hired Dawson (Dawson depo., 46:2-6); Uber determined which drivers were offered which rides (Tolkin Depo., 85:17-23); Uber provided guidelines for when Dawson should be on duty (Tolkin Depo., 79:15-80:7; 100:9-103:12); Uber set ride acceptance standards, cancellation rates, and could terminate drivers (Tolkin Depo., 38:10-45:22); Dawson could not take hails when responding to an Uber call, ask for a tip, or solicit independent rides (Tolkin Depo., 106:8-10; 109:19-110:1); Uber tracked the rides, collected the fares, and paid Dawson a percentage (Dawson Depo., 81:19-82:17; Tolkin Depo., 58:24-61:22); and Uber had an "Onboarding Script" that controlled Dawson's conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: WAN LING SONG VS. TOMMY ZHONG YANG FANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Tommy Fang, And Go Lorrie'S Airport Shuttle'S Motion For Protective Order To Limit Number Of Plaintiff'S Disclosed Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, Line 12, Defendants Tommy Fang, And Go Lorrie'S Airport Shuttle's Motion For Protective Order To Limit Number Of Plaintiff's Disclosed Expert Witnesses. Granted. Plaintiff is limited to the ten specific non-retained experts listed in her supplemental briefing (Masak, Argand, Sakamoto, Mindea, Vasudevan, Park, Valerie Lee, Win, Euguene Lee, and Alexander), with sanctions awarded consisting of paying for the costs of the depositions of any expert depositions defendant Lorrie chooses to take beyond the five retained experts and the first five non-retained experts. The costs are limited to expert fees and court reporter costs, not attorney's fees. This Order is without prejudice to any orders the trial judge may make regarding experts. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW KUMIN VS. STEPHEN SOMMERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Plaintiff Matthew Kumins Requests For Admissions, Set One Deemed Admitted And For A Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday June 24, 2015, Line 3 - PLAINTIFF MATTHEW KUMIN's Motion For Order Deeming Plaintiff Matthew Kumins Requests For Admissions, Set One Deemed Admitted And For A Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Margaret Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion of Plaintiff Matthew Kumin for an order deeming requests for admission admitted and for monetary sanctions is GRANTED. Requests for Admission Nos. 1 - 3 in Plaintiff's Requests for Admission, Set One, to Defendant Stephen Sommers are deemed admitted. Defendant is ordered to pay Plaintiff monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,560 within 20 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to margaret.niver@pillsburylaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANNLIA PAGANINI HILL VS. SILVIO MANISCALCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Silvio Maniscalco'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, Line 11, Defendant Silvio Maniscalco's Demurrer To Complaint. The demurrer to the complaint is overruled. Defendants have 5 days to answer. Defendant raises a factual issue that is outside the four corners of the complaint. The complaint and judicially noticeable documents do no clearly disclose what, if anything, plaintiff served on defendant "[w]ithin 15 days of delivery of a Subsection (f)(1) notice of intent to the Rent Board¿" Paragraph 13 of the complaint alleges the ultimate fact that defendant complied with all of the mandates of state and local law and the court must take this allegation as true. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
